records.ety: [F. coin, formerly also coing, wedge, stamp, corner, fr. L. cuneus wedge; prob. akin to E. cone, hone. See Hone, n., and cf. Coigne, Quoin, Cuneiform.]

defs: A quoin; a corner or external angle; a wedge. See Coigne, and Quoin.
A piece of metal on which certain characters are stamped by government authority, making it legally current as money; -- much used in a collective sense.
quotes: It is alleged that it [a subsidy] exceeded all the current coin of the realm.

defs: That which serves for payment or recompense.
quotes: The loss of present advantage to flesh and blood is repaid in a nobler coin.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Coined (koind); p. pr. & vb. n. Coining.]

defs: To make of a definite fineness, and convert into coins, as a mass of metal; to mint; to manufacture; as, to coin silver dollars; to coin a medal.
To make or fabricate; to invent; to originate; as, to coin a word.
quotes: Some tale, some new pretense, he daily coined, To soothe his sister and delude her mind.

defs: To acquire rapidly, as money; to make.
quotes: Tenants cannot coin rent just at quarter day.
They cannot touch me for coining.
